name: clawbureau
description: Clawsig Privacy Assurance v1 — fail-closed egress, sensitivity handling, processor policy, privacy proof UX

userStories.title: Fail-closed outbound allowlist for proofed runs
userStories.description: Add deny-by-default outbound policy for proofed runs. Only explicitly allowlisted destinations (Clawproxy + approved internal services) may be reached. Direct provider endpoints and arbitrary third-party egress must fail closed.
acceptanceCriteria: Proofed mode defaults to deny-by-default outbound traffic
Allowed destinations are configurable via policy
Direct provider endpoints are rejected in proofed mode
Blocked outbound attempts return deterministic reason codes
Integration test proves unauthorized host egress is blocked

userStories.title: Proxy-only model execution in proofed mode
userStories.description: Force proofed runs to route model traffic through Clawproxy only. If the proxy path is unavailable or a provider would be called directly, fail closed instead of silently downgrading provenance/privacy guarantees.
acceptanceCriteria: Proofed mode routes Google/OpenAI/Anthropic/OpenRouter traffic through Clawproxy only
Direct provider calls fail closed in proofed mode
Proofed child processes do not rely on raw provider access outside the proxy path
Gateway-tier proofs are impossible without proxy mediation
Regression tests cover at least one real proofed provider path

userStories.title: Signed egress policy receipt
userStories.description: Emit a signed receipt describing the egress policy in force for the run: allowed destinations, whether direct providers were blocked, and whether any blocked attempts occurred. This becomes canonical privacy evidence instead of requiring reviewers to infer posture from raw net events.
acceptanceCriteria: Proof bundle carries a signed egress policy receipt or equivalent canonical attestation
Receipt records policy version or hash
Receipt records whether direct provider access was blocked
Receipt records blocked attempt counts
clawverify validates the receipt and fails closed on malformed policy evidence

userStories.title: Minimal proofed sandbox profile
userStories.description: Define a minimal runtime profile for privacy-sensitive proofed runs with reduced ambient process/network noise. The goal is to make privacy telemetry interpretable and reduce side-path risk from desktop/browser helper processes.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated proofed sandbox/runtime profile exists
Baseline process inventory is captured at run start
Proofed mode documents the difference between dev mode and privacy-assurance mode
Runtime profile reduces ambient browser/helper noise versus current ad hoc execution
Fallback behavior is explicit when the profile cannot be activated

userStories.title: Runtime hygiene receipt + CLDD noise budget
userStories.description: Convert raw runtime telemetry into a signed runtime hygiene receipt with clear verdicts: interpose active, baseline hash, unmediated/unmonitored counts, and whether the run stayed inside the acceptable noise budget.
acceptanceCriteria: Proof bundle carries a signed runtime hygiene receipt
Receipt includes sandbox/profile identity and baseline process metadata
Raw CLDD signals are bucketed into good/caution/action verdicts
clawsig prove can distinguish background noise from reviewer-action-required signals
Regression tests cover at least one noisy-environment classification path

userStories.title: Pre-egress sensitivity classifier
userStories.description: Classify outbound model payloads before egress for secrets, credentials, PII, customer-restricted content, and custom policy patterns. Privacy assurance needs to know what class of data would leave the machine before deciding whether to allow, redact, or block it.
acceptanceCriteria: Classifier runs before outbound model requests in proofed mode
Default rule set covers secrets, credentials, PII, and customer-defined patterns
Classifier returns deterministic class/rule IDs for matched content
Unknown/unsupported classifier states fail closed or require explicit override
Tests cover positive and negative matches for at least secrets + PII

userStories.title: Block/redact/approval action engine + data-handling receipt
userStories.description: Add a policy action engine for sensitive classes: allow, redact, block, or require approval. Emit a signed data-handling receipt so reviewers can see what classes were found and what action was taken.
acceptanceCriteria: Each sensitive class maps to allow/redact/block/require-approval
Redacted payloads are the actual payloads sent to the provider
Blocked payloads fail closed with deterministic reason codes
Proof bundle carries a signed data-handling receipt with class list + action taken
clawverify validates the data-handling receipt

userStories.title: Processor policy engine + processor receipt
userStories.description: Enforce approved provider/model/region/retention combinations for proofed runs. Emit signed processor policy evidence so reviewers can see whether data went only to approved processors under the configured policy profile.
acceptanceCriteria: Policy supports provider/model allowlists
Policy supports region and retention-profile constraints
Disallowed processor routes fail closed in proofed mode
Proof bundle carries a signed processor policy receipt
clawsig prove surfaces the approved processor profile used for the run

userStories.title: Privacy posture report in clawsig prove
userStories.description: Upgrade `clawsig prove` to render privacy posture clearly: allowed processors used, blocked destinations, sensitive classes detected, actions taken, runtime hygiene, and reviewer action needed. The output should be readable by buyers/reviewers, not just engineers reading JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: Terminal + HTML proof reports include a privacy posture section
Report shows allowed processor(s), blocked egress attempts, sensitive classes, and data-handling actions
Report distinguishes background noise from action-required privacy signals
JSON output includes machine-readable privacy verdicts
Sample proof report can explain what is proven vs what is not proven

userStories.title: Privacy/compliance export pack
userStories.description: Produce an auditor-facing export pack that bundles proof bundle evidence, policy receipts, and a clear statement of privacy claims and non-claims. This is the artifact a buyer/security reviewer should attach to tickets, approvals, or postmortems.
acceptanceCriteria: Single command emits a privacy/compliance export pack
Export pack includes proof bundle plus privacy policy receipts
Export pack includes a human-readable 'what is proven / not proven' summary
Artifact is stable enough to attach to security reviews and disputes
Docs explain how reviewers should interpret the pack
